mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.5 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of observations where UNRATE exceeds 6.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive equity returns over the next month through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a statistically significant positive mean return (p > 0.05) over the subsequent 20 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index during late-cycle expansions reflect capacity constraints and profit margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth in late-cycle regimes yields positive 60-day forward returns in >50% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) outside early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 2.0 percent impose financing constraints on highly leveraged sectors, triggering negative equity returns over the next month as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0 percent, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor demand, generating immediate positive equity returns over the next week through momentum trading.
failure_condition: Concurrent PAYEMS growth and UNRATE declines produce negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during post-shock recovery phases drives corporate earnings upgrades, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as valuation multiples expand.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth in recovery regimes fails to generate positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the predictive coefficient turns negative during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the industrial production index signal expanding manufacturing output and higher corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO changes coincide with negative 20-day forward returns in >60% of observations.
